title: Stepper Item
description: A numeric-family variable widget rendering increment/decrement buttons around a numeric value. It binds one document/container variable (the `variable` it sets) and lets a viewer nudge a numeric value up or down; on change the value flows through the resolver's override path and re-resolves the document so dependent `${var}`/`$var` consumers update live. A stepper may only bind a variable whose declared type is `number` or `integer` — the resolver enforces this widget↔variable type compatibility and reports WIDGET_TYPE_MISMATCH otherwise. Optional `min`/`max` bound the value and `step` sets the per-click increment; the resolver rejects an inverted range (min > max) or a non-positive step. The variable itself is declared in the document/container `variables`, which supplies the effective default; this config only declares the binding plus its presentation. A stepper never has children.

constraints.description: The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
Inclusive lower bound for the entered value — editable at runtime. Must not exceed max.
Inclusive upper bound for the entered value — editable at runtime. Must not be less than min.
Increment between selectable values — editable at runtime. Must be a positive number.

variable.description: Name of the variable this widget sets. Must be a variable visible in the widget's scope (declared at document scope or on an ancestor container) and declared with a type the widget's family permits (number family → number or integer). The value chosen here becomes that variable's runtime override on re-resolve.

label.description: Optional label rendered next to the control.

description.description: Optional help text describing what the input controls, rendered beneath or beside the control.

default.description: Optional widget-local default shown before the viewer steps the value. The variable's declared default remains the authoritative resolution-time value; this is presentation only.

disabled.description: When true the control is rendered read-only and cannot be stepped.

min.description: Optional inclusive lower bound for the value. Must not exceed max.

max.description: Optional inclusive upper bound for the value. Must not be less than min.

step.description: Optional increment applied per step. Must be a positive number.
